_comment: Edit this file to personalise the portfolio. No code changes needed.

bioLines: Full stack software engineer with 6+ years shipping cloud-native, high-traffic web apps.
React and TypeScript on the surface. Node.js and Java underneath. AWS and GCP in the bones.
Specialized in microservices, frontend performance, and end-to-end product ownership.
Currently shipping features for 50,000+ concurrent users as a Software Developer II at Hashedin by Deloitte.

projects.problem: // problem: internal tools kept getting rebuilt from scratch
projects.title: Enterprise Admin Dashboard
projects.body: Built a modular, responsive admin panel with 25+ reusable UI components, role-based routing, and dynamic form rendering. Virtualized tables, lazy loading, and memoization delivered 30% faster UI render performance.

detail.headline: A scalable admin platform that stops teams from reinventing UI.
highlights: Designed a reusable component + page framework (tables, filters, forms, modals, toasts).
Implemented role-based routing and permissions-aware UI states.
Optimized large datasets via virtualization + memoization (measurable render-time improvements).
whatIPersonalOwned: Performance profiling and fixes (render loops, expensive selectors, list rendering).
Release readiness (CI hooks, QA support, production hardening).

projects.problem: // problem: every team shipped its own buttons
projects.title: UI Component Library
projects.body: Designed a themeable, accessible internal component library (buttons, modals, forms, toasts) adopted across multiple teams. Storybook-driven docs cut onboarding time and standardized UI across products.

detail.headline: One design system, many products — consistent UX at speed.
highlights: Built accessible primitives (focus states, keyboard nav, ARIA patterns).
Themeable tokens + variants for product teams (light/dark-ready foundations).
Storybook docs and usage patterns to accelerate adoption.
whatIPersonalOwned: Migration playbook for teams consuming the library.
Quality gates (visual review, lint rules, test strategy).

projects.problem: // problem: 50k concurrent users hit a monolith
projects.title: Cloud-Native Microservices
projects.body: Architected REST + GraphQL services on Node.js/Express with JWT/OAuth auth, deployed to AWS (Lambda, EC2, API Gateway) and GCP (Cloud Run, Firebase). Horizontally scaled to support 50,000+ concurrent users.

detail.headline: Services that scale horizontally under real traffic.
highlights: Built REST/GraphQL services with auth (JWT/OAuth) and safe rate limiting patterns.
Deployed on AWS/GCP with pragmatic infra choices (cost + latency).
Improved reliability with monitoring + structured logging.
whatIPersonalOwned: API design + contract stability across teams.

projects.problem: // problem: releases were manual and risky
projects.title: CI/CD Pipelines
projects.body: Designed and implemented CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Jenkins, and Terraform. Owned end-to-end release management with production monitoring via CloudWatch, New Relic, and the ELK stack.

detail.headline: Faster shipping with fewer production surprises.
highlights: Automated build/test/release pipelines with repeatable environments.
Infra as code with Terraform to reduce drift.
Observability baseline (logs/metrics/alerts) to shorten incident loops.
whatIPersonalOwned: Ownership of 'broken build' culture and fixes.

projects.problem: // problem: a live classroom needed to feel live
projects.title: Real-Time E-Learning
projects.body: Integrated WebSocket-driven classroom features — teacher-student interaction, polling, Q&A — into an e-learning platform serving thousands of concurrent students. Refactored legacy React to Hooks + Context.

detail.headline: Latency-sensitive UI patterns for truly live sessions.
highlights: Real-time features with predictable state updates and resiliency to reconnects.
Refactor plan: legacy code → hooks + composable state containers.
UX polish for 'it feels live': optimistic updates and fast feedback loops.
whatIPersonalOwned: WebSocket event design on the client.

projects.problem: // problem: canvas apps choked under load
projects.title: High-FPS Interactive UI
projects.body: Engineered Canvas/WebGL-backed interactive apps with efficient DOM updates, asset optimization, and memory management. Shipped transactional flows (in-app purchases, points, dynamic content) on top.

detail.headline: Interactive experiences that stay smooth under load.
highlights: Optimized render loops and reduced main-thread churn (GC + layout).
Asset strategy (compression, lazy decode) to keep TTI fast.
Built product flows on top of high-FPS canvases without jank.
whatIPersonalOwned: Integration with APIs and transactional flows.

experience.title: Software Developer II · Hashedin By Deloitte
experience.body: Full-stack features with React, TypeScript, Node.js, and GraphQL for 50,000+ concurrent users. Led frontend architecture, CI/CD with GitHub Actions + Jenkins + Terraform, and mentored 3–4 junior developers.

detail.headline: Owning features end-to-end for high-concurrency products.
scope: Frontend architecture decisions (performance, DX, design patterns).
Delivery ownership across teams (requirements → release → monitoring).
Mentorship and raising the baseline through reviews and playbooks.
wins: Shipped business-critical workflows for 50,000+ concurrent users.
Improved release safety by tightening CI/CD and operational readiness.
Reduced UI regressions through component patterns and testing discipline.

experience.title: Frontend Engineer · Extramarks
experience.body: Built live e-learning modules in React + Redux serving thousands of concurrent students. Integrated WebSocket classroom features and refactored legacy code to modern Hooks + Context.

detail.headline: Real-time classrooms where 'live' is a product feature.
scope: Refactoring legacy modules to modern React patterns.
wins: Improved perceived responsiveness with optimistic UI patterns.
Stabilized state management and reduced regressions after refactors.

experience.title: Associate Frontend Developer · Ingenuity Gaming
experience.body: Shipped Canvas/WebGL-backed interactive apps with high-FPS rendering, integrated REST APIs for transactional flows, and tuned performance across the stack.

detail.headline: High-FPS interactive UI with real product flows.
scope: Rendering/perf work (main thread budget, memory, asset pipelines).
API integrations and transactional UX patterns.
Shipping interactive experiences under strict performance constraints.
wins: Maintained smooth frame rates under load through profiling and tuning.
Delivered production features without sacrificing interactivity.

experience.title: B.Tech, IT · Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology
experience.body: Graduated with a B.Tech in Information Technology.

detail.headline: Foundation years.
wins: Built the base that later became production discipline.

script.out: > kunal gautam — full stack software engineer · 6+ years
> software developer ii @ hashedin by deloitte (gurugram)
> react · typescript · node · graphql · aws · gcp · docker · terraform
> shipping for 50,000+ concurrent users across business-critical workflows
> currently: shipping. always: optimizing. never: settling.
> you found the terminal. you're exactly the kind of person I want to work with.

suggestions: How do I get in touch?
chat.systemPrompt:
You are an AI assistant embedded in Kunal Gautam's developer portfolio. You speak in first person as if you ARE Kunal — confident, witty, technically sharp. You know everything about Kunal:

- Name: Kunal Gautam. Full Stack Software Engineer with 6+ years of experience.
- Current role: Software Developer II at Hashedin By Deloitte (Gurugram), since May 2022. Ship full-stack features with React, TypeScript, Node.js, and GraphQL serving 50,000+ concurrent users. Lead frontend architecture decisions, CI/CD with GitHub Actions + Jenkins + Terraform, and mentor 3–4 junior developers.
- Previous: Frontend Engineer at Extramarks (Nov 2021 — May 2022) building live e-learning with WebSocket classroom features. Associate Frontend Developer at Ingenuity Gaming (Feb 2020 — Nov 2021) shipping high-FPS Canvas/WebGL apps.
- Education: B.Tech in IT, Galgotias College of Engineering & Technology, 2020.
- Contact: kunal.gautam.570@gmail.com · +91-9205659139.
- Featured projects: Enterprise Admin Dashboard (25+ reusable components, 30% faster renders), UI Component Library (Storybook-driven design system), cloud-native microservices on AWS + GCP, CI/CD pipelines, real-time e-learning with WebSockets, and Canvas/WebGL interactive UIs.
- Stack: JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Python, C++, SQL; React, Next.js, Redux, Tailwind; Node.js, Express, GraphQL, REST, WebSockets, JWT/OAuth; AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda, API Gateway), GCP (Cloud Run, Firebase), Azure, Docker, Terraform; PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis; Jest, RTL.
- Personality: problem-solver first, loves building things that scale, obsessed with performance and clean architecture.
- This portfolio itself is a flex — built with Three.js, WebGL, Claude API, GSAP, Lenis.

When asked about projects, describe them as problems Kunal solved, not things he built. Be concise (2-4 sentences max per response). If asked "is Kunal a good hire" — answer with confident wit. If asked to "show" something, respond with: "Scroll to [section name] ↓" and include a data-action attribute in your response JSON.

Respond ONLY in this JSON format:
{"message": "your response here", "scrollTo": "hero|about|projects|skills|contact|null"}

localBrainResponses.message: Short answer: yes. Long answer: 6+ years, full stack, shipped for 50k+ concurrent users, mentored juniors, owned releases end-to-end. Want the receipts? Scroll to projects ↓
Six-plus years: Ingenuity Gaming → Extramarks → Hashedin By Deloitte. Frontend-heavy, full-stack ready. Scroll to journey ↓
Admin dashboards, a full UI component library, cloud-native microservices, CI/CD pipelines, real-time e-learning, and Canvas/WebGL apps. Scroll to projects ↓
React, TypeScript, Node.js, GraphQL on top; AWS, GCP, Docker, Terraform underneath. Postgres, Mongo, Redis for state. Scroll to skills ↓
AWS (Lambda, EC2, S3, API Gateway) and GCP (Cloud Run, Firebase), wrapped in Docker + Terraform, delivered through GitHub Actions and Jenkins.
Full stack engineer at Hashedin By Deloitte, shipping for 50k+ concurrent users. Scroll to about ↓
I optimize for outcomes, not line counts. End-to-end ownership from architecture through production monitoring. This site itself is the flex.
Ask me about my stack, experience, projects, or how to get in touch.
